methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Aalo Atomics is a Series A–stage nuclear energy company that has raised $30M+. The valuation universe comprises 8 private peers (including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power) and 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR), though the narrow peer set for same-stage and similar-tier analysis is limited.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($30M) as a proxy for post-money Series A valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $60M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient datapoints within narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier subsets all fell below the three-company threshold needed for reliable median calculation), though the broader nuclear sector comps provide directional context.

The triangulation analysis, derived from the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $51M (low) to $69M (high), with a median of $60M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer multiples at this stage and the nascency of the nuclear energy venture ecosystem, which limits historical precedent for step-up rate calibration. Investors should weight this range against technology maturity, capital efficiency, and regulatory pathway clarity.
